standard.name: ICNIRP 2013
standard.organization: International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection
standard.notes: Skin exposure limits only. Ocular limits are not included.

CA.description: Wavelength correction factor for 400–1400 nm skin MPE (Table 3, p. 282)

uv_discrete_steps.description: UV photochemical MPE for 302–315 nm (Table 5). Discrete 1-nm steps. Values in J/cm². Each entry gives the upper wavelength boundary and the MPE below that boundary.
uv_discrete_steps.note: Left-inclusive, right-exclusive. For λ in [302, 303): H = 4e-3, for [303, 304): H = 6e-3, etc.

thermal.description: UV thermal limit: 5.6 t^0.25 kJ/m² = 0.56 t^0.25 J/cm². Listed as 'Also not to exceed' in Table 5.

regions.note: 10 kJ/m² = 1.0 J/cm² for t ≥ 10 s. Below 10 s: photochemical undefined, only thermal applies.

bands.name: Visible/Near-IR
bands.note: Table 7, p. 285. All sub-regions multiply by C_A.

regions.note: 11 C_A t^0.25 kJ/m² = 1.1 C_A t^0.25 J/cm²
2.0 C_A kW/m² = 0.2 C_A W/cm² → H = 0.2 C_A t J/cm²

t_max.description: Recommended maximum anticipated exposure durations for skin. ICNIRP 2013 specifies Tmax as 'the maximum anticipated time of direct exposure' (p. 286) without prescribing specific values for skin. The values below are conservative engineering guidance for typical scenarios, not directly from a specific ICNIRP 2013 table.

limiting_apertures.description: Limiting aperture diameters for skin MPE averaging (Table 8, Skin column).
limiting_apertures.small_beam_note: Table 7 note b: 'For beam diameters less than 1 mm, the actual radiant exposure, i.e., not averaged over of the limiting aperture of 3.5 mm, should be compared to the exposure limit.' This is a general measurement directive that applies to all exposure evaluations including repetitive-pulse and scanning scenarios.
limiting_apertures.small_beam_reference: Table 7 note b; Measurement section p. 288
limiting_apertures.beam_diameter_definition: For Gaussian beams, beam diameter is the 1/e diameter: the distance between diametrically opposed points where irradiance is 1/e (37%) of peak.

large_area_correction.description: MPE correction for large exposed skin areas (λ > 1,400 nm, t > 10 s). Table 7 note c, p. 285; also p. 281. For exposed areas between 0.01 m² (100 cm²) and 0.1 m² (1000 cm²), the exposure limit is adjusted proportionally to the inverse of the exposed area. For exposed skin areas exceeding 0.1 m², the exposure limit is 100 W m⁻² (10 mW/cm²).
large_area_correction.note: E_limit = 10/A_s W/m² where A_s is exposed skin area in m² (equivalently 10000/A_s mW/cm² where A_s in cm²). For A_s ≥ 0.1 m² (1000 cm²): E_limit = 100 W/m² (10 mW/cm²). ICNIRP 2013 note c and p. 281 use the phrases 'beam cross-sectional areas' and 'area of the exposed skin' in the context of stationary large-beam exposures. The standard does not explicitly address how this correction applies to scanning beams.

uv_successive_day_derate.description: De-rating factor for UV (280–400 nm) on successive days.

irradiance_primary.description: Conditions under which the standard expresses MPE primarily as irradiance rather than radiant exposure.

repetitive_pulse_skin.description: ICNIRP 2013 repetitive pulse rules for skin exposure (p. 287). Rules 1 and 2 apply. Rule 3 (Cp correction factor) is specified 'for the retinal thermal limits' only and does NOT apply to skin.
repetitive_pulse_skin.rule_1: The exposure from any single pulse shall not exceed the EL for a single pulse of that pulse duration.
repetitive_pulse_skin.rule_2: The exposure from any group of pulses delivered in time T shall not exceed the EL for time T. T varies from pulse duration to total exposure duration.
repetitive_pulse_skin.rule_3_note: Rule 3 (Cp = n^-0.25 correction) applies only to retinal thermal limits, not to skin. ICNIRP 2013 p. 287: 'For the retinal thermal limits, an additional factor Cp is applied...'
